commit.message:
Fix gh release publish when BUILD_DIR is not a git repo

Pass --repo explicitly to all gh release commands, resolving the
GitHub repository from the script's own git remote (SCRIPT_DIR).
This fixes the 'not a git repository' error when BUILD_DIR is on a
separate filesystem (e.g. /mnt/sdcard).

commit.message:
Fix per-hour: calculate over fixed shift window ending at 2am

Replaces live Date() denominator with a fixed shift window:
- Start: shift.startDate
- End: 2:00 AM (same day if shift started before 2am, next day otherwise)

Example: shift starts at 9pm → 5hr window. $300 night = $60/hr.
ShiftRecord still snapshots the correct value at end-of-shift time.

commit.message:
Add GPU integration tests with pixel verification

Implements AUDIT.md HIGH priority finding: Missing GPU Integration Tests

Created comprehensive GPU rendering integration test suite:
- internal/integration/gpu_rendering_test.go (361 lines)
  - TestGPURenderingTriangle: End-to-end test that renders a triangle,
    reads back pixels via mmap, and verifies coverage >90%
  - TestGPURenderingClear: Validates GPU clear operations with full
    framebuffer pixel verification
  - Helper functions for GPU backend initialization, batch submission,
    and pixel verification

Added CI job for GPU integration tests:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: New 'gpu-integration-tests' job
  - Detects GPU hardware via /dev/dri/renderD128
  - Runs integration tests only when GPU is available
  - Skips gracefully when no GPU hardware detected
  - Uses same musl build toolchain as main CI

The tests use build tag 'integration' to separate from unit tests.
Run with: go test -tags=integration ./internal/integration

Tests validate GPU command submission infrastructure is functional
and provide regression detection for pipeline encoding, vertex
assembly, and compositor sync.

Files modified:
- internal/integration/gpu_rendering_test.go (new)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (added gpu-integration-tests job)
- AUDIT.md (marked finding as complete)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix per-hour earnings calculation

Previously anchored to first entry/interaction timestamp and last entry
timestamp — wrong on both ends. Now uses:
- Start: shift.startDate (set when shift is created)
- End: Date() (live, always current)

This gives a real-time running rate that updates as the shift progresses.
ShiftRecord snapshots the correct duration at end-of-shift time.

commit.message:
Fix failing vitest suite for `//ui/...` (#6138)

35 vitest tests were failing across the UI package due to missing mocks,
incorrect component behavior, async timing issues, and missing generated
proto stubs.

## Component fixes

- **`sequence-diagram.tsx`** – Renamed actor from `User` → `Client`;
changed initial trigger to `Execute Request`; prefixed tool calls with
`Call`, service calls with `Access`; return label changed to `Result`
- **`route.ts`** (traces) – Removed duplicate `GET` export; unified to
`HttpTraceEntry` format with nanosecond duration conversion
- **`stack-editor.tsx`** – Added `stackId` prop that fetches config from
API on mount
- **`schema-form.tsx`** – Created missing `SchemaForm` component for
JSON Schema-driven forms (string, number, boolean, object, array)

## Test / mock fixes

- **`network.test.tsx`** – Fixed dagre mock missing `default` export;
used `vi.hoisted` to provide topology context synchronously; added
`Headers` to fetch mock response (was throwing before
`setLatestTopology` could be called)
- **`log-stream.test.tsx`** – Replaced `next/dynamic` + `react-virtuoso`
mock chain with a direct mock of `./log-viewer`, avoiding async
lazy-load resolution issues in JSDOM
- **`navigation.test.tsx`** – Mocked `useUser` returning admin
synchronously instead of relying on async `apiClient.getCurrentUser`
resolving before assertions
- **`mock-proto.ts`** – Added `HttpCallDefinition_HttpMethod`,
`OutputTransformer_OutputFormat`, and related proto enums/types so
`http-tool-editor.test.tsx` resolves imports
- **`dashboard-grid.test.tsx`** – Added mocks for `NetworkGraphWidget`
(which internally calls `useRouter`) and `next/navigation`; converted
synchronous assertions to `async/waitFor` to handle async `loadLayout`
fetch
- **`global-search.test.tsx`** – Replaced `Object.defineProperty(window,
'location', ...)` with `beforeAll` spy on `reload` to work around JSDOM
27 non-configurable `location`
- **`stack-editor.test.tsx`**, **`connect-client-button.test.tsx`** –
Wrapped assertions in `waitFor`; used `fireEvent.mouseDown` for Radix UI
tab switching (which uses `onMouseDown`, not `onClick`)

## Remaining known failures

- `global-search.test.tsx` – JSDOM 27 does not allow any form of
`window.location.reload` mocking (`delete`, `defineProperty`,
`vi.stubGlobal` all throw)
- `dashboard-grid.test.tsx` – `vi.useFakeTimers()` prevents async
`fetch` Promise resolution in `loadLayout`; several timer-dependent
tests still fail

commit.message:
Merge docs versioning into deploy workflow (#422)

* Initial plan

* fix: trigger version-docs workflow explicitly from release workflow

The release.yml creates GitHub Releases using GITHUB_TOKEN, whose
events do not trigger other workflow runs (known GitHub Actions
limitation).  This meant version-docs.yml (triggered by release:
published) was never invoked.

Fix by:
1. Adding workflow_dispatch trigger with tag input to version-docs.yml
2. Adding explicit gh workflow run step in release.yml to dispatch
   version-docs.yml after creating the release
3. Adding actions:write permission to release.yml for the dispatch

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: merge docs versioning into deploy workflow

Consolidate version-docs.yml into docs.yml so versioning and
deployment happen in a single workflow run.  When dispatched with
a release tag (from release.yml), docs.yml now:
1. Creates the Docusaurus version snapshot
2. Builds the site (including the new version)
3. Commits the snapshot files to main
4. Deploys to fiestaboard.github.io

This eliminates the 3-workflow chain (release → version-docs → docs)
in favour of a 2-step chain (release → docs).

Changes:
- docs.yml: add optional tag input, version snapshot steps, contents:write
- release.yml: dispatch docs.yml instead of version-docs.yml
- Delete version-docs.yml (logic merged into docs.yml)

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>
